---
title: "Research"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-title: "On this page"
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: article
---

## Peer-reviewed articles & book chapters

- Scarazzato, L., Liang, X., Tian, N., & Lopes da Silva, D. (2024). [Developments in Arms Production and the Effects of the War in Ukraine](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10242694.2024.2381784). *Defence and Peace Economics*, 1–21.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2023). [Political Accountability and Military Spending](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10242694.2022.2129128). *Defence and Peace Economics*, 34(5), 563–580.
- Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., Béraud-Sudreau, L., Scarazzato, L., Liang, X., & Assis, A. (2023). [Developments in Military Expenditure and the Effects of the War in Ukraine](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10242694.2023.2221877). *Defence and Peace Economics*, 34(5), 547–562.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2020). [Arms for Export? A Reappraisal of the Brazilian Arms Industry](https://www.epsjournal.org.uk/index.php/EPSJ/article/view/330). *Economics of Peace and Security Journal*, 15(1), 31–38.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2019). Reassessing Brazil's Arms Industry. In K. Hartley & J. Belin (Eds.), *The Economics of the Global Defence Industry*. New York: Routledge.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2018). [Filling Arms Production Data Gaps: South America as a Case in Point](https://www.epsjournal.org.uk/index.php/EPSJ/article/view/313). *Economics of Peace and Security Journal*, 13(2), 20–27.


## SIPRI & partner publications

- Liang, X., Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., Scarazzato, L., Karim, K., & Guiberteau Ricard, J. (2025). [Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2024](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2025/sipri-fact-sheets/trends-world-military-expenditure-2024). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., Liang, X., & Scarazzato, L. (2024). Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2023. SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Scarazzato, L., Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., Liang, X., & Djokic, K. (2024). The SIPRI Top 100 Arms-producing and Military Services Companies, 2023. SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., Liang, X., Scarazzato, L., Béraud-Sudreau, L., & Assis, A. (2023). [Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2022](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2023/sipri-fact-sheets/trends-world-military-expenditure-2022). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Liang, X., Scarazzato, L., Béraud-Sudreau, L., Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., Choi, Y., & Kristjan Sild, E. (2023). [The SIPRI Top 100 Arms-producing and Military Services Companies, 2022](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2023/sipri-fact-sheets/sipri-top-100-arms-producing-and-military-services-companies-2022). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2023). [Using Taxation to Fund Military Spending](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2023/sipri-insights-peace-and-security/using-taxation-fund-military-spending). SIPRI Insights on Peace and Security.
- Lopes da Silva, D., Tian, N., Béraud-Sudreau, L., Marksteiner, A., & Liang, X. (2022). [Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2021](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2022/sipri-fact-sheets/trends-world-military-expenditure-2021). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Béraud-Sudreau, L., Liang, X., Lopes da Silva, D., Tian, N., & Scarazzato, L. (2022). [The SIPRI Top 100 Arms-producing and Military Services Companies, 2021](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2022/sipri-fact-sheets/sipri-top-100-arms-producing-and-military-services-companies-2021). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Lopes da Silva, D., Tian, N., & Marksteiner, A. (2022). [Pathways for Reducing Military Spending in Post-civil Conflict Settings](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2022/sipri-insights-peace-and-security/pathways-reducing-military-spending-post-civil-conflict-settings). SIPRI Insights on Peace and Security.
- Lopes da Silva, D., Tian, N., & Marksteiner, A. (2021). [Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2020](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2021/sipri-fact-sheets/trends-world-military-expenditure-2020). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Marksteiner, A., Béraud-Sudreau, L., Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., & Kuimova, A. (2021). [The SIPRI Top 100 Arms-producing and Military Services Companies, 2020](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2021/sipri-fact-sheets/sipri-top-100-arms-producing-and-military-services-companies-2020). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Tian, N., Lopes da Silva, D., & Kuimova, A. (2020). [Military Spending and the Achievement of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development](https://www.un.org/disarmament/publications/occasionalpapers/unoda-occasional-papers-no-35-april-2020/). In *UNODA Occasional Papers no. 35: Rethinking Unconstrained Military Spending* (pp. 21–37). United Nations, New York.
- Tian, N., Kuimova, A., Lopes da Silva, D., Wezeman, P. D., & Wezeman, S. T. (2020). [Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2019](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2020/sipri-fact-sheets/trends-world-military-expenditure-2019). SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Tian, N., & Lopes da Silva, D. (2020). [Military Spending and Official Development Assistance in Recipient States: Is there a Relationship?](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2020/sipri-insights-peace-and-security/military-spending-and-official-development-assistance-recipient-states-there-relationship-0) SIPRI Insights on Peace and Security.
- Béraud-Sudreau, L., Marksteiner, A., Lopes da Silva, D., Tian, N., Kuimova, A., Wezeman, P. D., & Wezeman, S. T. (2020). [Mapping the International Presence of the World's Largest Arms Companies](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2020/sipri-insights-peace-and-security/mapping-international-presence-worlds-largest-arms-companies). SIPRI Insights on Peace and Security.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2020). [Emerging Suppliers in the Global Arms Trade](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2020/sipri-insights-peace-and-security/emerging-suppliers-global-arms-trade). SIPRI Insights on Peace and Security.
- Wezeman, P. D., Fleurant, A., Kuimova, A., Lopes da Silva, D., Tian, N., & Wezeman, S. T. (2020). Trends in International Arms Transfers, 2019. SIPRI Fact Sheet.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2019). [The SIPRI Top 100 Arms-producing and Military Services Companies, 2018](https://www.sipri.org/publications/2019/sipri-fact-sheets/sipri-top-100-arms-producing-and-military-services-companies-2018). SIPRI Fact Sheet.

## SIPRI Yearbook chapters

- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2025). Military expenditure. *SIPRI Yearbook 2025*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2025). Arms production and military services. *SIPRI Yearbook 2025*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2024). Military expenditure and developments in arms production. *SIPRI Yearbook 2024*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2023). Military expenditure and arms production. *SIPRI Yearbook 2023*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2022). [Military expenditure](https://www.sipri.org/yearbook/2022/08). *SIPRI Yearbook 2022*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2021). [Military expenditure](https://www.sipri.org/yearbook/2021/08). *SIPRI Yearbook 2021*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2020). [Military expenditure](https://www.sipri.org/yearbook/2020/08); [International arms transfers and developments in arms production](https://www.sipri.org/yearbook/2020/09). *SIPRI Yearbook 2020*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2018). [Military expenditure](https://www.sipri.org/yearbook/2018/04). *SIPRI Yearbook 2018*.
- Lopes da Silva, D. et al. (2017). [Military expenditure](https://www.sipri.org/yearbook/2017/09). *SIPRI Yearbook 2017*.

## Commentary & essays (SIPRI)

- Lopes da Silva, D. (2025). Before it's too late: Why a world of interacting AI agents demands new safeguards. SIPRI Essay.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2023). [Iraq since the invasion: 20 years in SIPRI data](https://www.sipri.org/commentary/topical-backgrounder/2023/iraq-invasion-20-years-sipri-data). SIPRI Topical Backgrounder.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2020). [Autocracy is on the rise: Should we expect military spending to follow?](https://www.sipri.org/commentary/topical-backgrounder/2020/autocracy-rise-should-we-expect-military-spending-follow) SIPRI Topical Backgrounder.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2019). [Ending off-budget military funding: Lessons from Chile](https://www.sipri.org/commentary/topical-backgrounder/2019/ending-budget-military-funding-lessons-chile). SIPRI Topical Backgrounder.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2019). [The crucial role of the military in the Venezuelan crisis](https://www.sipri.org/commentary/topical-backgrounder/2019/crucial-role-military-venezuelan-crisis). SIPRI Topical Backgrounder.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2017). [Improving South American military expenditure data](https://www.sipri.org/commentary/topical-backgrounder/2017/improving-south-american-military-expenditure-data). SIPRI Topical Backgrounder.

## Earlier publications (Portuguese)

- Lopes da Silva, D. (2016). Dependência da Importação de Insumos para a Defesa: Uma Breve Análise do Caso Colombiano. *Revista Brasileira de Estudos de Defesa*, 3(1).
- Saint Pierre, H. L., & Lopes da Silva, D. (2014). A Torre de Babel Sul-Americana: A Importância da Convergência Conceitual para a Cooperação em Defesa. In É. C. A. Winand, I. R. Barnabé, & L. M. Pinheiro (Orgs.), *Relações Internacionais* (pp. 43–66). Brasília: Fundação Alexandre de Gusmão.
- Saint Pierre, H. L., & Lopes da Silva, D. (2014). Percepções de Segurança Regional no Âmbito da UNASUL: O Conselho Sul-Americano de Defesa. In L. F. Ayerbe (Org.), *Territorialidades e Entrecruzamentos Geopolíticos na América Latina* (Vol. 1, pp. 220–244). São Paulo: Cultura Acadêmica.
- Lopes da Silva, D. (2014). Metodologias de Transparência Militar: Aportes Técnicos e Teóricos sobre o Caso Sul-Americano. *Cadernos de Campo*, 2(1).
